spec_match.rationale: The live drag test observes the drag source event sequence, while the replay panel is explicitly labelled as a counterfactual comparison rather than a support probe.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the replay buttons, the real drag chip/drop zone, and the clear button, each of which updated the visible DOM state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature blurb by contrasting the Chrome 147+ suppression sequence with the observed fallback path for older or non-supporting behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now separates the real browser drag result from the legacy counterfactual and shows fallback cleanup code for browsers that do not dispatch pointercancel after dragstart.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/6732314958757888 link plus spec and WPT references.

design_system.rationale: A current static scan found no raw hex colors, inline styles, or inline handlers, and local button background overrides set explicit text colors.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 at /v147/pointer-event-suppression-on-drag-start/compatibility-lab/?verify=pointer-replay-fix. The page loaded with the ChromeStatus link and exact pointercancel handler probe; Replay old behavior changed the sequence to missing pointercancel/pointerout/pointerleave, Replay Chrome 147+ restored the firing sequence, DevTools drag of DRAG ME to the drop zone produced a real event log and reported the observed fallback path as NO - older behaviour, and Clear reset the log/verdict. Console and network were clean. The feature conformance route showed 7 PASS / 0 FAIL.
